title: Lieberman's Last-Mover Advantage in Sequential Auctions
description: A flowchart illustrating Lieberman's analysis of how a second mover can guarantee a win in a two-item, sequential, second-price auction by exploiting the first mover's bid.
elements: Player 1 bids b1 on Item A
Final Outcome: Player 1 wins A (pays b2=0), Player 2 wins B (pays 0)
relationships: Player 1 acts first by bidding b1 on Item A
Player 2 observes Player 1's bid and then acts
Player 2's strategy is a direct reaction to Player 1's bid
Player 2's calculated bid on Item A forces a specific outcome
The sequence leads to a subgame perfect equilibrium where the second mover gains an advantage
